name: Nairobi National Museum
description: A major museum showcasing Kenya’s history, culture, nature, and contemporary heritage collections.

name: Kenya National Archives
description: A public archive and museum preserving important historical records, photographs, and cultural artifacts.

name: Nairobi Gallery
description: An art and heritage gallery located near the city center, featuring exhibitions and Kenyan cultural collections.

name: Railway Museum Nairobi
description: A museum dedicated to the history of rail transport in Kenya and East Africa, with locomotives and railway artifacts.

name: Uhuru Park
description: A large public park in central Nairobi known for open green space, recreation, and city views.

name: Karura Forest
description: An urban forest reserve popular for walking trails, cycling, waterfalls, caves, and nature experiences.

name: Nairobi Arboretum
description: A green recreational park and botanical site with walking paths, picnic areas, and a variety of tree species.

name: Nairobi National Park
description: A wildlife park located just outside the city center, known for game viewing with Nairobi’s skyline in the background.

name: Giraffe Centre
description: A conservation and education center where visitors can learn about and feed endangered Rothschild giraffes.

name: Sheldrick Wildlife Trust
description: A well-known elephant orphan rescue and rehabilitation center focused on wildlife conservation and education.

name: Bomas of Kenya
description: A cultural center showcasing traditional Kenyan homesteads, music, dance, and heritage from different communities.

name: Uhuru Gardens National Monument
description: A national monument and public historical site associated with Kenya’s independence and national heritage.
